name: proof-guided-web-change-app
notes: The owned specimen server binds only to an ephemeral 127.0.0.1 port.
The shell and each specimen require an ephemeral 256-bit run capability; proof-target authorization is injected into the Playwright context and excluded from semantic targets and retained artifacts.
The application resolver accepts only app-issued opaque candidate references.
Only two exact app-owned specimen source variants may execute, and display runtimes are separate from fresh proof-target runtimes.

The local runtime directly owns loopback networking, artifact filesystem access, ephemeral signing, specimen execution, and the Playwright subprocess; the proof client/provider remains the verification seam.
The deterministic repair executor receives source bytes and meaning-level findings, never proof audit material.
The application has no hosted Riddle client or configuration.
